Branding · Advertising · Visual Design

Skincare Advertising
for Slova

A suite of advertising banners for Slova Skincare — a brand built around the belief that skincare should be uncomplicated and purely focused on your skin. The creative direction balances clean, considered aesthetics with the warmth and trust that the category demands — making every banner feel as considered as the products it represents.

Client

Slova Skincare

Role

Visual Designer

Deliverables

8 advertising banners

Scope

Branding Advertising Visual Design

01 — Creative Direction

Skincare that only cares
for your skin

Slova's brand proposition is built on simplicity and purity — products that strip away everything unnecessary and focus entirely on what the skin actually needs. The advertising creative had to embody this philosophy visually: clean compositions, purposeful negative space, and imagery that communicates efficacy without clinical coldness.

The challenge in skincare advertising is a familiar one — standing out in a saturated category without resorting to overclaiming. Every banner in this suite was designed to feel credible and considered, letting the product and its packaging do the talking within a thoughtfully composed frame.

Clean visual hierarchy — Product-forward compositions with structured typographic layouts that guide the eye without competing with the imagery.

Consistent brand language — A unified colour palette and typographic system across all 8 banners, ensuring the suite reads as a cohesive campaign rather than individual executions.

Multi-format adaptability — Compositions designed to translate across digital display and print contexts without losing their visual integrity.

Design principle: In beauty advertising, restraint is a statement. A clean, uncluttered banner communicates confidence in the product — the design steps back so the product steps forward.

02 — Advertising Banners

Eight executions,
one visual language

Each banner in the suite serves a distinct purpose within the campaign — from hero brand awareness executions to product-specific feature banners — while maintaining the consistent visual identity that makes Slova immediately recognisable across every touchpoint.

The approach

"Good advertising for a skincare brand doesn't shout — it reassures. Every composition, every choice of space and colour, is in service of a single idea: that this brand understands your skin, and trusts you to recognise quality when you see it."
